learning_outcomes.description: Explain the interrelationships between all the aspects of the fire safety strategy for buildings and infrastructure (e.g. tunnels, transportation)
Apply the concepts of emergency evacuation, fire growth, compartment fire dynamics, detection, and active and passive fire protection measures to real problems in fire safety engineering
Quantify the contribution of the elements of a fire safety strategy to available and required safe egress time
Explain the different approaches to provision of fire safety in the built environment.
Identify the consequences of poor incorporation of fire safety in building and infrastructure.

assessments.task_description:
This is an assignment which comprises a range of problem solving and short answer questions, which covers the following contents:

  • Introduction to Fire Safety Engineering.
  • Egress
  • Fire science I
  • Fire science II
  • Compartment fire dynamics I

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. 

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. 

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The written responses to the questions should by scanned and submitted on Turnitin
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The exam will include problems and short-answer questions. The exam will cover all parts of the course.

his assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
